Dates on each article: 13 July 2026 / 2026-07-13, matching the actual run date, in both the visible byline and the JSON-LD datePublished/dateModified; this is correct on every new post across eleven consecutive runs (2-13 July), directly satisfying the client's standing instruction that article dates must always reflect the real publish date. IMPORTANT CORRECTION to this file's own prior notes: before sending this run's Slack notification, the actual channel history (#build-seo-pages) was read back via the Slack MCP tool rather than trusted from this log. It showed that despite ten consecutive prior run-log entries claiming plain bare URLs had been standing practice since 2 July and were 'confirmed working', the messages actually sent almost every day used Slack's <url|label> bracket syntax, and on at least one occasion (12 July, the fractional-sales-leadership-for-startups link) the visible label was long enough that Slack truncated it with an ellipsis, which is what the client has been seeing as a broken-looking link. This run first tried sending genuinely bare URLs with nothing else on the line; reading the message back showed Slack itself still auto-wraps any bare URL into its own <url|label> bracket form for display, using the URL text as the label, which stayed close to the same truncation width (57-60 chars against a ~55-65 char threshold observed from the 12 July failure). The confirmed, truncation-proof fix, sent as a correction in the same run and verified by reading it back clean: plain markdown [short title](url) with a short human-readable title as the label (not the URL text), one per line, blank line between entries. Because the label is short regardless of how long the slug is, there is nothing for Slack to truncate. Use this [title](url) markdown format for every future run's Slack notification; do not use bare URLs (Slack re-wraps them with the URL itself as the label) and do not use Slack's own <url|label> bracket syntax directly (same truncation risk on long slugs). The 21 legacy pillar/money pages from the original June launch batch still carry their original June 2026 dates; this remains out of scope for this authority-only 3-post routine (raised by the client seven times now, including as an explicit instruction on 12 and 13 July) and stays flagged as a standalone bulk backdate-correction job for the client to schedule separately, since this 3-post routine has no mechanism to touch pillar/money pages.
